title: iOS 26 gate not enforced in AIOrchestrator — Foundation Model called unconditionally
description: FoundationModelService.isAvailable is never checked before calling adapt(). The @available annotation prevents compilation on older OS but if DI injects the service without the guard, raw private health data flows into an undefined path.
evidence: AIOrchestrator.process() contains no #available(iOS 26, *) guard at any call site
recommended_fix: Add guard foundationModel.isAvailable else { ... } before every adapt() call, or wrap in #available(iOS 26, *)

title: Misleading docstring — claims confidence 0 escalates to cloud, but Foundation Model is Tier 3 (post-cloud)
description: FallbackFoundationModel.adapt() returns confidence 0.0 with comment saying orchestrator will escalate to cloud. But Foundation Model is Tier 3 — cloud was already called at Tier 2. The 0-confidence result keeps the unpersonalised baseRecommendation.
evidence: Protocol docstring and FallbackFoundationModel comment both incorrect
recommended_fix: Correct both to say confidence 0 → orchestrator keeps unpersonalised baseRecommendation

title: Header says 'encrypted store' but uses plain UserDefaults — security misrepresentation
description: Class header reads 'On-device encrypted store of recommendation outcomes.' Actual persistence is UserDefaults.standard — no encryption beyond standard iOS file protection. Stored signals are health-adjacent inferences.
recommended_fix: Use Keychain-backed store or NSFileProtection.completeUnlessOpen, or remove 'encrypted' from header

title: genderIdentity hardcoded to prefer_not_to_say — profile value discarded
description: snapshot.genderIdentity is unconditionally set to 'prefer_not_to_say'. Users who provided gender during onboarding are silently excluded from gender-segmented cohorts.
recommended_fix: Read profile.genderIdentity and map to valid values, fallback to prefer_not_to_say only when nil

title: trainingDaysPerWeek is compile-time constant, not user's actual schedule
description: DayType.allCases.filter(\.isTrainingDay).count counts enum cases, not the user's weekly plan. User training 2 days gets same value as one training 5 days.
recommended_fix: Source from user's actual program schedule or UserPreferences.trainingDaysPerWeek

title: dietPattern hardcoded to 'standard' — user diet preference never used
description: Every user's nutrition bands include diet_pattern: standard regardless of actual diet. Vegetarian, vegan, keto users misclassified.
recommended_fix: Map from UserPreferences diet field, standard as nil fallback only

title: stressLevel() fabricates 'moderate' when no log exists — suppresses insufficientData path
description: Non-optional return type means nil log returns 'moderate', causing recovery segment to always have a stressLevel. Suppresses insufficientData path and sends fabricated band to cloud.
evidence: Returns "moderate" when no recent log exists
recommended_fix: Return String? and nil when no log present. Propagate nil through contribute()

title: BMI fallback uses startWeightKg — produces stale band for users who gained/lost weight
description: When live weight and recent log weight are nil, adapter uses onboarding start weight. For users with significant weight change, BMI band is wrong.
recommended_fix: Return nil when only startWeightKg available, trigger local fallback path

title: BMI has no plausibility bounds — heights of 1cm or weights of 1kg produce wildly wrong values
description: Only guard is heightCm > 0. Heights of 1cm or weights of 1kg pass and produce incorrect BMI, all falling in 30+ band.
recommended_fix: Add bounds: heightCm 100-250, weightKg 30-300

title: mealsPerDay falls back to total meal count including planned-but-uneaten meals
description: When no completed meals exist, mealsPerDay uses latestNutrition?.meals.count which includes planned uneaten meals. User with 5 planned but 0 eaten gets banded as 5+.
recommended_fix: Return nil when completed count is zero, propagate nil to trigger local fallback

title: lastAdapters empty on first process() — freshness penalized unfairly on first launch
description: lastAdapters initialized as []. First process() call before setAdapters() returns freshness 0, degrading first-launch recommendations to lower confidence tier.
recommended_fix: Build adapters inside process() from snapshot, or make setAdapters() a precondition

title: processAll() causes isProcessing to flicker true→false between segments — 4 loading states
description: Each process() owns its own isProcessing lifecycle via defer. processAll() toggles flag 4 times. SwiftUI observers see 4 loading states.
recommended_fix: Lift isProcessing lifecycle to processAll(), suppress in individual process() calls

title: JWT validation only checks structural format — expired JWTs cause avoidable network calls
description: looksLikeJWT accepts any x.y.z string. Expired JWTs pass, hit cloud, return 401.
recommended_fix: Add lightweight local expiry check on exp claim

title: localFallback GoalMode uses 'Fat Loss' string — mismatches 'weight_loss' from ProfileAdapter
description: NutritionGoalMode(rawValue: snapshot.primaryGoal ?? "Fat Loss") won't match weight_loss format. Both parse paths likely always fail, defaulting silently.
recommended_fix: Align string formats between ProfileAdapter and AITypes

title: trainingDaysWeekBand() has no case for 0 — rest weeks banded as 5+
description: Switch handles 1...2, 3...4, default 5+. A user with 0 training days (deload week) classified as high-frequency.
recommended_fix: Add case 0: return "0" or "rest_week"

title: enforceLimit() O(n × segments) under lock on every record() call
description: Four filter passes over entire outcomes array run synchronously under NSLock on every record(). OK at ~800 but doesn't scale.
evidence: Four full filter passes in enforceLimit()
recommended_fix: Use dictionary keyed by segment, O(1) eviction

title: avgSessionMinutes uses fixed 10min/exercise — systematically underestimates duration
description: max(20, log.exerciseLogs.count * 10) ignores sets, rest, logged duration. 6-exercise session estimated at 60min but actual is 75-90min.
recommended_fix: Use logged session duration when available, or a more accurate heuristic

title: Placeholder returns hardcoded confidence 0.8 — always passes confidence gate
description: Until iOS 26 SDK ships, applyLocalAdjustments returns 0.8 confidence. Since 0.8 >= 0.4 threshold, every iOS 26+ user gets placeholder with artificially high confidence.
recommended_fix: Add #warning and use lower placeholder confidence like 0.5

title: AnyCodable silently substitutes empty string for unrecognized JSON types
description: When no known type matches, value = "" is assigned silently. Consumers get unexpected empty strings.
recommended_fix: Throw DecodingError.dataCorruptedError to surface malformed payloads

title: build() discards adapters — callers must rebuild to call setAdapters()
description: Builder constructs adapters internally but returns only snapshot. Callers who need orchestrator.lastAdapters must duplicate construction. Root cause of AI-011.
recommended_fix: Return (snapshot, adapters) tuple from build()
